claims.scope: Degree-6 additive MBE made no strict joint false decision in the current generic conditional-null grid.
Final training loss is increment-supported in zero of 24 frozen PGDL metadata-floor fits.
The public Dziugaite source statistic matches its externally visible Figure 1 checks.
In the current 20-repetition refit grid, three null/proxy scenarios had zero strict support and the genuine increment had complete recovery.
In the frozen two-sample-size stress matrix, joint support was zero across null/proxy cells and complete across injected-signal cells; predictive-only false support was at most 5% in any cell.
Residual permutation controls nominal 5% error under clustered dependence.
claims.reason: The current clustered null rejected 7.0% of 500 repetitions, with a Wilson 95% interval of approximately 5.1-9.6%; residual permutation is diagnostic only.

claims.scope: MBE controls nominal error over the full realistic dependence class.
claims.reason: Current simulations are informative but finite and do not establish a general guarantee.

claims.scope: MBE is superior to CMI, granulated Kendall, and robust sign error.
claims.reason: The methods answer different estimands; the shared comparison is calibration, not universal dominance.

claims.scope: The selector recommends the best metric for arbitrary unseen tasks.
claims.reason: Prospective task-family transport and independent replication are not complete.

claims.scope: A second independent published metric study has been exactly reproduced and reaudited.
claims.reason: The Jiang, Fantastic Robustness Measures, and REF Complexity intakes do not expose the required run-level metric ledgers.

claims.scope: MBE identifies the causal effect of a metric on generalization.
claims.reason: Conditioning and predictive increment do not identify a causal metric effect.
